content: The mean deviation about the mean for the date $4,7,8,9,10,12,13,17$ is
Let $a, b, c, d$ and $e$ be the observations with mean $m$ and standard deviation $S$. The standard deviation of the observations $a+k$, $b+k, c+k, d+k$ and $e+k$ is
explanation:
$$\begin{aligned} &\text { The given observation are } a, b, c, d \text { and } e\\ &\begin{aligned} & \quad \text { Mean }=m=\frac{a+b+c+d+e}{5} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \Sigma x_i=a+b+c+d+e=5 m \\ & \therefore \text { Standard deviation, } S=\sqrt{\frac{\Sigma x_i^2}{5}-m^2} \end{aligned} \end{aligned}$$

Now, consider the observations $a+k, b+k, c+k$, $d+k, e+k$.

Now, mean

$$\begin{aligned} & \text { mean } \\ & =\frac{(a+k)+(b+k)+(c+k)+(d+k)+(e+k)}{5} \\ & =\frac{a+b+c+d+e+5 k}{5} \\ & =\frac{5 m+5 k}{5}=m+k \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \text { New standard devaition }=\sqrt{\frac{\sum\left(x_i+k\right)^2}{5}-(m+k)^2} \\ & \\ & =\sqrt{\frac{\sum\left(x_i^2+k^2+2 x_i k\right)}{5}-\left(m^2+k^2+2 m k\right)} \\ & \\ & =\sqrt{\frac{\sum x_i^2}{5}-m^2+\frac{5 k^2}{5}-k^2+\frac{2 k \Sigma x_i}{5}-2 m k} \\ & \\ & =\sqrt{\frac{\sum x_i^2}{5}-m^2+\frac{2 k \times 5 m}{5}-2 m k} \\ & \\ & =\sqrt{\frac{\sum x_i^2}{5}-m^2}=S \end{aligned}$$

content: The mean of 100 observations is 50 and their standard deviation is 5. Then, the sum of squares of all observations is
explanation:
We know, standard deviation,

$$\begin{aligned} P^2 & =\frac{\Sigma x^2}{n}-(\bar{x})^2 \\ P & =5, \Sigma x=50, n=100 \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad 25=\frac{\Sigma x^2}{100}-(50)^2 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \Sigma x^2=252500 \end{aligned}$$

Thus, sum of squares of all observation in 252500.

content: If the standard deviation of the numbers $$-1, 0,1, k$$ is $$\sqrt{5}$$ where $$k>0$$, then $$k$$ is equal to
explanation:
Given, numbers are $$-1,0,1, k$$.

Standard deviation, $$\sigma=\sqrt{5}$$

$$\sigma^2=\frac{\Sigma x_i^2}{n}-\left(\frac{\Sigma x_i}{n}\right)^2$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad 5=\frac{1+0+1+k^2}{4}-\left(\frac{-1+0+1+k}{4}\right)^2 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 5=\frac{2+k^2}{4}-\frac{k^2}{16} \Rightarrow 80=8+4 k^2-k^2 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 72=3 k^2 \Rightarrow k^2=24 \Rightarrow k=2 \sqrt{6} \end{aligned}$$

content: The standard deviation of the numbers $$31,32,33 \ldots 46,47$$ is
explanation:
Given, numbers : $$31,32,33, \ldots, 46,47$$.

The standard deviation of $$31,32,33, \ldots, 47$$ will be the same as those of $$1,2,3, \ldots, 17$$. (decreasing each item by 30)

$$\because \quad \mathrm{SD}=\sqrt{\frac{n^2-1}{12}}$$

Here, $$\quad n=17$$

$$\begin{aligned} \therefore \quad \mathrm{SD} & =\sqrt{\frac{17^2-1}{12}}=\sqrt{\frac{288}{12}} \\ & =\sqrt{24}=2 \sqrt{6} \end{aligned}$$

content: The standard deviation of the data 6, 7, 8, 9, 10 is
Mean and standard deviation of 100 items are 50 and 4 , respectively. The sum of all squares of the items is
explanation:
Key Idea Use mean $$(\bar{x})=\frac{\sum_\limits{i=1}^n x_i}{n}$$ and standard deviation (S.D) $$=\sqrt{\frac{\sum_\limits{i=1}^n x i^2}{n}-\left(\frac{\sum_\limits{i=1}^n x i}{n}\right)^2}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \text { Given, } \frac{\sum_\limits{i=1}^{100} x i}{100}=50 \\ & \begin{aligned} & \text { and }=\sqrt{\frac{\sum_\limits{i=1}^{100} x i^2}{100}-\left(\frac{\sum_\limits{i=1}^{100} x i}{100}\right)^2}=4 \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{\sum_\limits{i=1}^{100} x i^2}{100}-(50)^2=(4)^2 \Rightarrow \sum_\limits{i=1}^{100} x i^2=100(2500+16) \\ &=251600\end{aligned}\end{aligned}$$

content:
For the probability distribution given by
         $$ \begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|} \hline X=x_i & 0 & 1 & 2 \\ \hline P_i & \frac{25}{36} & \frac{5}{18} & \frac{1}{36} \\ \hline \end{array} $$
the standard deviation $(\sigma)$ is
explanation:
$$\text { Probability distribution given by }$$
$$X = {x_i}$$ $$P$$ $${{P_i}{x_i}}$$ $${{P_i}x_i^2}$$
0 $${{25} \over {36}}$$ 0 0
1 $${5 \over {18}}$$ $${5 \over {18}}$$ $${5 \over {18}}$$
2 $${1 \over {36}}$$ $${2 \over {36}}$$ $${4 \over {36}}$$
$$\sum {{P_i}{x_i} = {1 \over 3}} $$ $$\sum {{P_i}x_i^2 = {7 \over {18}}} $$
$$ \begin{aligned} &\text { Standard deviation }\\ &\begin{aligned} \sigma & =\sqrt{\Sigma P_i x_i^2-\left(\Sigma P_i x_i\right)^2} \\ & =\sqrt{\frac{7}{18}-\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^2}=\sqrt{\frac{7}{18}-\frac{1}{9}} \\ & =\sqrt{\frac{5}{18}}=\frac{1}{3} \sqrt{\frac{5}{2}} \end{aligned} \end{aligned} $$

content: If coefficient of variation is 60 and standard deviation is 24 , then Arithmetic mean is
explanation:
We are given:

Coefficient of Variation (CV) = 60

Standard Deviation ($\sigma$) = 24

To find the Arithmetic Mean ($\bar{x}$), we use the formula for the Coefficient of Variation:

$ CV = \frac{\sigma}{\bar{x}} \times 100 $

Substituting the given values:

$ 60 = \frac{24}{\bar{x}} \times 100 $

To solve for $\bar{x}$, rearrange the equation:

$ \bar{x} = \frac{24 \times 100}{60} $

Simplifying the expression:

$ \bar{x} = 40 $

Therefore, the Arithmetic Mean is 40.
